creator: a@amilanov.com
text:
Hi Johan,

I thought more about it and I agree that this would oversimplify the 
situation. I'm sorry.

Best wishes,
Alexander

On 07/15/2013 02:44 PM, Johan van Selst wrote:
> Hi Alexander,
>
> I'm not happy with the current licence framework in ports. Adding the
> suggested licence information may confuse users and may suggest
> unwarranted legal implications.
>
> This port uses a BSD licence, but this is a *different* BSD licence than
> the version used by the FreeBSD base system. Also, the port links with
> the Berkeley DB library, which uses different licences, depending on the
> exact version deployed. BDB recently switched to the AGPL. It is not
> clear what the exact licence of the resulting binary would be under
> various legal systems.
>
> As a port maintainer, I prefer not to add assertions that might be
> interpreted as legal advice to users. Especially when I feel that this
> assertion could be misleading.
>
>
> Regards,
> Johan
